title: RankTransformOfArray
description:
Given an array of integers, replace each element with its rank. The rank is how large the element is compared to others, starting from 1 for the smallest element. If two elements are equal, they share the same rank. Return a new array where each element is replaced by its rank.

Constraints:
- 0 ≤ arr.length ≤ 10⁵
- -10⁹ ≤ arr[i] ≤ 10⁹

testCases.description: Basic unsorted array with distinct values
All elements equal — same rank for all
Larger array with duplicates (two 12s share rank 3)

hints: Start by sorting the unique values.
Use a hashmap to map each value to its rank (index + 1).
Build the result by looking up each original element in the hashmap.

complexity.explanation: Sorting unique values takes O(n log n), then one pass to build the rank map and another to transform each element, both O(n). The rank map stores up to n entries.
